content.problemStatement:
Let's denote the length of the Longest Increasing Subsequence of the array BBB by LIS(B)\mathbf{LIS}(B)LIS(B).

For a permutation AAA of integers from 111 to NNN we can define two arrays of integers PPP and SSS, both of length NNN: Pi=LIS(A[1:i])=LIS([A1,A2,‚Ä¶,Ai])P_i = \mathbf{LIS}(A[1:i]) = \mathbf{LIS}([A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_i])Pi‚Äã=LIS(A[1:i])=LIS([A1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Ai‚Äã]), Si=LIS(A[i:N])=LIS([Ai,Ai+1,‚Ä¶,AN])S_i = \mathbf{LIS}(A[i:N]) = \mathbf{LIS}([A_i, A_{i+1}, \ldots, A_N])Si‚Äã=LIS(A[i:N])=LIS([Ai‚Äã,Ai+1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã]).

Let's call a pair of arrays (P,S)(P, S)(P,S)  viable\mathit{viable}viable, if there exists a permutation AAA with these arrays as PPP and SSS.

Given array PPP of length NNN, calculate the number of arrays SSS such that (P,S)(P, S)(P,S) is viable, modulo 998‚Äâ244‚Äâ353998\,244\,353998244353.


Input Format


The first line of input contains one integer NNN ‚àí-‚àí the length of the array PPP.




The second line of input contains NNN integers P1,P2,‚Ä¶,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1‚Äã,P2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,PN‚Äã.





Output Format
Print a single integer ‚àí-‚àí the answer to the problem modulo 998‚Äâ244‚Äâ353998\,244\,353998244353.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
It can be shown that the only array SSS, for which pair (P,S)(P, S)(P,S) is viable, is [3,2,1][3, 2, 1][3,2,1].

Explanation:
It can be shown that there is no permutation with such array PPP.
